performance_overview: Through 24 sessions (inception 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-29) Bull has completed 7 trades: 3W/4L (43% win rate, avg +0.15%/trade). Last 5 sessions (June 25-June 29) all flat with zero entries — 22 consecutive no-entry days. Cash preservation continues to outperform relative to ongoing SPY drawdown.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket; enforce hard ceiling at RSI 60; n=2 (directionally clean but sample is small — keep enforcing until n>=10)
avoid: RSI 60-65 at entry: 0/3 wins (0%), avg -1.06% — all overbought chase entries have lost; hard ceiling, no exceptions
RSI 65-75 at entry: 0/1 wins (0%), avg -0.61% — confirmed gap-up trap; skip entirely
Gap-up opens with RSI >60: zero qualifications across all 24 sessions; any name opening RSI >60 is a hard skip regardless of structural setup quality or conviction

Opening-bar RSI surge pattern: even premarket-clean RSI names (PANW 57, AMGN 58 premarket on June 29) surged to 70.9 and 70.8 on the opening bar — the gap-up trap is systematic and persistent; do NOT chase within first 5-10 minutes of open even if RSI looked clean premarket
Current watchlist universe (PANW, MGM, EIX, ES, D, AMGN): chronic ATR% compression 0.04-0.16% far below 0.30 floor; full universe refresh is highest-priority action before next entry session

The six-criteria gate (profitable + trailing-stop-active + above EMA-21 + no earnings within 2 days + sentiment not negative + 5% gap-risk cushion) has never been met simultaneously. Policy is sound and conservative — no change warranted. DOWN SPY regime increases overnight gap-down risk further.

notes_for_next_session:
FOR PREMARKET AGENT (Tue 2026-07-07 ~8:30 AM ET — first session after July 4th holiday weekend).

BOOK STATE: Flat. 22 consecutive no-entry sessions. Equity $99,938.80. 0 open positions.

HOLIDAY BREAK: July 3 (early close, no-trade day) and July 4 (Independence Day, market closed). No agents should run on these dates. Resume with full scan on July 7.

HIGHEST PRIORITY — UNIVERSE REFRESH: The current 6-name watchlist (PANW, MGM, EIX, ES, D, AMGN) is structurally broken and must be replaced before any entry attempt. All names have chronic ATR% compression (0.04-0.16%), far below the 0.30 entry floor. PANW is the lone exception (ATR% 0.357%) but disqualified every session on RSI. Run a fresh evening scan on July 6 (Sunday evening) or early July 7 using enhanced filters:
  1. ATR% > 0.35% at primary screen (raised from 0.30 to build cushion)
  2. Pre-market gap screen: flag names with >2.5% overnight gaps as elevated-gap-risk
  3. RS_20DAY > 1.10 (enforce)
  4. VCP ratio < 0.85 (enforce)
  5. Within 5% of 52-week high
  6. Avoid utilities sector — EIX, ES, D all failed ATR% floor repeatedly

SPY REGIME: DOWN (EMA-9 737.22 < EMA-21 740.10 as of June 29). Recheck regime at open July 7 — 9-day break may shift regime. Cap at 5 positions if still DOWN.

RSI GATE: Hard ceiling 60 — non-negotiable. RSI 55-60 is the ONLY winning bucket (2/2, 100%). Opening-bar surge pattern requires patience: do NOT buy first 5-10 minutes if RSI >60 even if setup otherwise perfect. Wait for RSI to reset <60 with volume confirmation before entry.

KEY INSIGHT: 22 consecutive no-entry sessions confirms the system filters are working correctly — the problem is candidate universe, not filter quality. A refreshed universe with ATR% >0.35% and pre-gap screening should unlock entries. If post-refresh still zero qualifications after July 7 open, escalate to a 100+ candidate scan across all sectors.
